# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_admin!

  def generate_report
    @query = params[:query]

    begin
      result = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(@query)
      @report_data = result.to_a
      render json: @report_data
    rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
      render json: { error: e.message }, status: :bad_request
    end
  end

  private

  def authenticate_admin!
    # Implement your admin authentication logic here
    head :unauthorized unless current_user&.admin?
  end
end
